'''Dave Dorman''' is an artist who has done covers for several ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comics]], books, and magazines. His major works include [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']], ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' and [[Star Wars: Young Jedi Knights|''Young Jedi Knights'']]. He also illustrated [[William C. Dietz]]' novella [[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]], for which he is credited as co-author. Dave contributed sketch cards for ''[[2007 Topps Star Wars 30th Anniversary]]''. He illustrated covers for the comic series ''[[Star Wars: Darth Maul — Death Sentence]]''.

{{Artist-stub}}

==Biography==
Dave Dorman was born in Michigan in [[1950s#1958|1958]] to a military family who lived several places including in the Azores and in Washington, DC. By the time he was in high school, Dave was a fan of the Flash Gordon serials and was intrigued by the thought of a big-budget film in that style. He graduated from a Maryland high school in [[1975]] and headed to [[Wikipedia:St. Mary's College of Maryland|St. Mary's College of Maryland]] as a Fine Arts major. While he was developing his skills in college, he was influenced by his exposure to Marvel comic books while his attraction to science fiction continued to shape his art.

As Dorman's exposure to art grew, he soon developed a love for painting while simultaneously keeping tabs on the film industry through magazines. He had heard of an upcoming movie called [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'']] so when he spotted a book by that name by [[George Lucas]] he eagerly bought it and read it.
<!-- Note to add this after the above paragraph gets fleshed out more: After two years at St. Mary's, he decided to attend the [[Wikipedia:Joe Kubert School of Cartoon and Graphic Art|Joe Kubert School of Comic Art]]. -->
